description: This is an optical micrograph of specimen BL, the longitudinal sample of Fe-29Mn-9.1Al-0.6C alloy (alloy B) after 1050 °C solution treatment for 1.5 h followed by oil quenching, with a 50 μm scale bar provided. The specimen microstructure comprises 90 vol% austenite matrix and 10 vol% string-like distributed ferrite phase. The image demonstrates that fatigue cracks, which initiate at slip bands only formed in austenite and propagate approximately perpendicular to the applied stress, are impeded by ferrite grains as the ferrite string arrangement is perpendicular to the crack propagation direction. This observation confirms that ferrite acts as a crack arrester in this orientation: the soft fine ferrite induces crack blunting at the initial fatigue stage and intense work hardening at the later stage to hinder crack propagation, explaining the superior fatigue performance of specimen BL over the transverse counterpart specimen BT.
